findings.title: North Star Metric: Daily Active Users Engaged in Collaboration
findings.description: The North Star metric is defined as the number of Daily Active Users (DAU) engaged in collaboration sessions, reflecting the core value of real-time collaboration features.
assumptions: Users engaged in collaboration sessions are likely to perceive value in the product.
recommendations: Monitor DAU engaged in collaboration sessions to assess feature adoption.

metadata.measurement_method: Daily tracking of active users participating in collaboration sessions.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Daily Collaboration Sessions
findings.description: This metric tracks the number of collaboration sessions initiated daily, serving as a primary indicator of user engagement with the real-time editing feature.
assumptions: Increased collaboration sessions indicate higher user engagement.
recommendations: Aim to increase daily collaboration sessions by enhancing user experience.

metadata.measurement_method: Daily tracking of collaboration sessions initiated.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Concurrent Edit Attempts Blocked
findings.description: This metric tracks the number of concurrent edit attempts that are blocked, which should not increase as real-time collaboration features are implemented.
assumptions: A decrease in blocked attempts indicates improved collaboration functionality.
recommendations: Monitor this metric closely to ensure it does not increase post-launch.

metadata.measurement_method: Daily tracking of blocked concurrent edit attempts.

findings.title: Measurement Gap: User Feedback on Collaboration Experience
findings.description: There is insufficient data on user feedback specifically related to the collaboration experience, which is critical for understanding user satisfaction and areas for improvement.
assumptions: User feedback is essential for iterating on collaboration features.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise customers asking for simultaneous editing

recommendations: Implement user surveys and feedback mechanisms focused on collaboration features.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: A/B Testing Collaboration Features
findings.description: Conduct A/B tests on different implementations of real-time collaboration features to determine which version yields higher user engagement and satisfaction.
assumptions: Different implementations may lead to varying levels of user engagement.
recommendations: Set statistical parameters for A/B testing: 95% confidence level, 80% power.

summary: The analysis identifies a North Star metric focused on daily active users engaged in collaboration, supported by primary KPIs and guardrail metrics. Gaps in user feedback measurement and opportunities for A/B testing collaboration features were also highlighted to enhance data-driven decision-making.
